Japanese: ‥‥尾並田さんが、あなたを 吾童川へつき落とした‥‥
English: You say that Mr. Fawles pushed you into the Eagle River...

Japanese: それは、あり得ないの。
English: However, that's hard to believe.

Japanese: で、でも！ わたし、背中を押されて‥‥
English: B-But it's true! I felt a push on my back...

Japanese: まちがいありません！ あれは、尾並田さまでしたわ！
English: I'm certain of it! It was Mr. Fawles!

Japanese: どうやら‥‥私の言い方が 悪かったようね。
English: I'm sorry... I guess I wasn't clear enough.

Japanese: 《あり得ない》というより‥‥ むしろ、《不可能》だったのです。
English: I shouldn't have said, "that's hard to believe." I should've said, "That's impossible."

Japanese: ふ、《不可能》‥‥？
English: I-Impossible...?

Japanese: 事件の舞台となった、おぼろ橋‥‥ 思い出してごらんなさい。
English: I ask that the court recall the condition of Dusky Bridge, now and 5 years ago.

Japanese: ５年前の現場の状況は、 現在とまったく変わっていません。
English: That bridge hasn't changed one bit in these last 5 years.

Japanese: ‥‥もし、あなたの言うとおり、 背中を押されたとしたら‥‥？
English: If someone had pushed you from behind as you have claimed...

Japanese: ‥‥そこには、川は流れていない。 岩場が広がっているだけ‥‥
English: ...instead of being carried away by the river...

Japanese: あなたは急流に飲みこまれることも なく、岩場にたたきつけられて‥‥
English: ...you would have been smashed by the bedrock below!

Japanese: カクジツに死んでいたはずです。
English: A most certain death.

Japanese: ‥‥おわかりでしょう？ 美柳　ちなみさん‥‥
English: Do you understand now, Dahlia Hawthorne?

Japanese: 尾並田　美散が、あなたを つき落とすのは、不可能だった！
English: The very notion that my client pushed you from behind is impossible!

Japanese: きゃああああああッ！
English: Aaaaaah!

Japanese: 事件が起こったのは、 もう５年も前のことだ！
English: Your Honor, this event occurred 5 years ago!

Japanese: 吾童川は‥‥現在よりも水位が 高かったかもしれないではないか！
English: Why, for all we know, the water level in the river may have been higher back then.

Japanese: 橋から水面までの高さは、 １２メートルもあります！
English: But it's 40 feet from the bridge to the river!

Japanese: 多少の水位のちがいなど、 モンダイになりません！
English: A small change in the water level wouldn't have made a difference!

Japanese: ぐううううッ！
English: Ugggh!

Japanese: た‥‥たしかに！
English: Y-You're right! If the events occurred just as the witness has testified...

Japanese: 被告人には、証人を つき落とすことができなかった‥‥
English: ...then the defendant couldn't have pushed the witness into the river.

Japanese: おじょうさん！ どういうことですかな‥‥？
English: Young lady! What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: ‥‥ううッ！
English: Uuuh!

Japanese: わ‥‥わたし‥‥あの。 それは‥‥
English: I... I... I, err... You see, I...

Japanese: ‥‥お待ちいただこう、 裁判長！
English: Just a moment, Your Honor!

Japanese: たしかに、彼女は証言した。 『犯人につき落とされた』‥‥と。
English: It's true that the witness testified that the defendant pushed her into the river...

Japanese: しかし！　落下ポイントが 《背後》だったとは言っていない！
English: However! She never stated that she fell from the back end of the bridge!

Japanese: な‥‥、何を言い出すの！
English: What... What do you mean?

Japanese: 右腕を撃たれた尾並田　美散は、 橋の上でパニックになった！
English: After being shot in the arm, it's plausible that Mr. Fawles panicked!

Japanese: そして‥‥証人を、 橋のワキから、つき落としたのだ！
English: Therefore, he could have unwittingly pushed her off the side of the bridge!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥それなら、 ちゃんと吾童川に落下します！
English: If that's true, she would have fallen into the river!

Japanese: いかがですか、証人！ 御剣検事くんのセツメイは‥‥
English: Well, Miss Hawthorne!? Is Mr. Edgeworth's explanation correct...?

Japanese: そういえば‥‥
English: Now that you mention it...

Japanese: ぼんやり、思い出しました。 橋から落ちた瞬間‥‥
English: I do remember now. When I fell off the bridge...

Japanese: ワキのワイヤーに、スカートが 引っかかった記憶がありますわ！
English: ...my skirt got caught on one of the bridge's side wires!

Japanese: なんですってェェッ！
English: You can't be serious!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛にィッ！
English: Order! Order in the court!

Japanese: ‥‥どうやら、弁護士クンの 攻撃も、ここまでのようだ‥‥
English: It seems Ms. Fey's assault has finally reached its conclusion.

Japanese: （ダメ‥‥千尋！　ここで 引くワケにはいかないわ‥‥！）
English: (Not now, Mia...! This is no time to retreat!)

Japanese: ザンネンですが‥‥弁護側の 攻撃は、ここからです！
English: Unfortunately for you... this is just the start of Ms. Fey's assault!

Japanese: な‥‥なんだと！
English: Wh-What?

Japanese: ‥‥あなたのセツメイは、 こうでしたね？　検事さん。
English: I believe your reasoning went something like this, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: “右腕を撃たれた尾並田さんは、 橋の上でパニックになった”
English: "After being shot in the arm, it's plausible that Mr. Fawles panicked!"

Japanese: “そして‥‥証人を、 橋のワキから、つき落とした”
English: "Therefore, he could have unwittingly pushed her off the side of the bridge!"

Japanese: ‥‥しかし！　それはさらに 《不可能》なのです！
English: However! Once again, I'm forced to say, "That's impossible!"

Japanese: な‥‥何を、バカなッ！ いったい、なぜだ！
English: R-Ridiculous! What's so impossible about it!?

Japanese: あなたの、今の主張には‥‥ 決定的なムジュンがあります！
English: Because your flawed logic contradicts the Court Record!

Japanese: この証拠品こそが、 決定的なミスですっ！
English: This evidence shows the fatal error you've made!

Japanese: たしかに。‥‥決定的なミスだ。
English: It's a fatal error, alright.

Japanese: もちろん‥‥キミ自身の、だが。
English: Unfortunately... The error is yours, not mine.

Japanese: （“キミ”と来たか‥‥）
English: (Uh-oh, here it comes...)

Japanese: ‥‥弁護人。
English: Ms. Fey.

Japanese: ここまで来たら、もう ヨウシャしませんぞッ！
English: At this point in the trial, I can't just let that pass!

Japanese: ううう‥‥。
English: Ouchie.

Japanese: （むずかしく考えないで、千尋！ もう一度、考えなおすの！）
English: (Come on, Mia! Take your time and think it over again!)

Japanese: 裁判長！　‥‥すべてのコタエは この写真にあります！
English: Your Honor! All of the answers are right here in this photo!

Japanese: おぼろ橋を両ワキで支えている ワイヤーを見てください！
English: Take a look at the wires supporting both sides of the bridge!

Japanese: 肩の高さ‥‥１．５メートルまで、 しっかり守られている‥‥
English: They extend up to about five feet off the ground...

Japanese: ここから“つき落とす”なんて、 アキラカに《不可能》です！
English: It would be impossible to push someone off from there!

Japanese: いやああああああああああああッ！
English: NNNNOOOOOOO!

Japanese: ひ‥‥‥被告人の体格を 思い出していただきたい！
English: B-But let's remember the size and strength of the defendant!

Japanese: 彼ならば、こんなワイヤーなど モンダイにならない！
English: Wires like this wouldn't be a problem for him!

Japanese: １４才の少女を抱え上げ、 投げ落とすことができたはずだ！
English: He could have easily picked up a 14 year old girl and thrown her over!

Japanese: ‥‥検事さんこそ、お忘れですか？
English: So young, and already so forgetful, Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 尾並田さんは、 右腕を撃たれていたのです。
English: Mr. Fawles had been shot in the right arm.

Japanese: そして、さらに重要なことは‥‥
English: And more importantly...

Japanese: 彼は、ピストルで狙われていた。 至近距離から‥‥勇希さんに！
English: ...Valerie Hawthorne had her gun trained on him... at point-blank range!

Japanese: ム‥‥ッ！
English: Ugh...!

Japanese: 尾並田さんが、証人を投げ落とす？ ‥‥そんなこと、不可能なのです！
English: So! Mr. Fawles throwing the witness off the bridge? That is clearly impossible!

Japanese: ぐおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: GWAAAAAAAHHH!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！ そ、それでは‥‥どういうコトに！
English: Order! Order! Wh-What is the meaning of this!?

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみさん！
English: Dahlia Hawthorne!

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、吾童川に 自分から飛びこんだのです！
English: You jumped into the Eagle River intentionally!

Japanese: な‥‥ なんですとおおォォッ！
English: What...!? What is this...!?

Japanese: な‥‥何を言うかッ！ なぜ、そんなバカげたコトを‥‥！
English: Indeed! What do you mean by such a ridiculous remark!?

Japanese: そ、そうですわ！
English: Y-Yes! It's ridiculous!

Japanese: わたしには、お姉さまが ついていたのです！
English: My sister was there to help me!

Japanese: ピストルと‥‥手錠も持って。 わたしを助けてくれたのに‥‥
English: She had her gun and handcuffs. She could have saved me...

Japanese: あんな急流に自分から 飛びこむなんて、自殺行為ですわ！
English: Jumping into a raging river like that... That would have been suicide!

Japanese: それでも‥‥あなたは 身を投げたのです！
English: Perhaps... But still, that's exactly what you did!

Japanese: きっと、急流を泳ぎきる 自信があったのでしょう。
English: You were probably confident that you could handle the swift current.

Japanese: そして、何よりも‥‥
English: But even more so...

Japanese: 証人には、飛びこまなければ ならない理由があったのです！
English: ...the witness had a much more compelling reason for jumping into the river!

Japanese: いったい、なんだというのか！ ‥‥その、理由とはッ！
English: Oh? Then what was it!? What was so important that she'd want to jump into the river!?

Japanese: “証人が生きていた”‥‥その ジジツが、すべてを物語っている！
English: The witness is still alive. This fact alone explains everything!

Japanese: あなたが、イノチをかけて 吾童川に身を投げた理由は‥‥！
English: This is why she risked her life by jumping into the rapids of the Eagle River!

Japanese: ‥‥お話にならない‥‥
English: That isn't even worth a reply.

Japanese: 弁護士クン。‥‥セキニンを とってもらおうか。
English: Ms. Fey. I hold you personally responsible...

Japanese: 審理を無用にコンランさせ、 証人をキズつけた‥‥ッ！
English: ...for confusing the court and causing great emotional pain to the witness!

Japanese: やむを得ませんな。 ペナルティを与えます。
English: I have no choice but to impose a penalty on you.

Japanese: （カンタンなことでしょ、千尋！ 彼女の《目的》‥‥）
English: (This is an easy one, Mia! What was it she was after...?)

Japanese: （きっと、あの検事さんだって 気づいているはず‥‥！）
English: (Even Edgeworth must have realized it...!)

Japanese: ‥‥今から、５年前‥‥
English: Five years ago...

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみとともに消えたものが もう１つ、ありました。
English: ...something else disappeared along with Dahlia that day.

Japanese: 《身代金》として犯人にわたった、 ２億円相当のダイヤ‥‥その原石！
English: The item that Valerie brought up the mountain with her... The 2 million dollar diamond!

Japanese: あああああああああッ！ ま‥‥‥まさか‥‥‥ッ！
English: AAAAAHHH!! N... No... It-It can't be...!

Japanese: すべては、最初から 仕組まれていたのです！
English: Yes. Dahlia had it all planned from the beginning!

Japanese: ２億円の《身代金》‥‥すべてを ひとりじめするためにッ！
English: The 2 million dollars... She was going to keep it all for herself!

Japanese: 尾並田さんに協力させて、 誘拐のシバイをして‥‥
English: She forced Mr. Fawles to help her fake the kidnapping...

Japanese: パァァァン！
English: *BANG!*

Japanese: 最後の瞬間‥‥彼をウラ切って、 あなたは急流に身を投げた！
English: At the last minute, she betrayed him and threw herself into the river...

Japanese: 自らの《身代金》を、背中の リュックサックにかくしたまま‥‥
English: ...with the ransom tucked away safely in her backpack...

Japanese: そんな‥‥そんな、バカなああッ！
English: Why that's... that's simply ridiculous!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order! Order!

Japanese: し、しかし！　５年前といえば‥‥ 証人は、まだ１４才だ！
English: Y-Your Honor! Five years ago, the witness was only 14 years old!

Japanese: ‥‥そんな、悪魔のような 計画など、実行できるわけが‥‥
English: Do you really think a 14 year old is capable of such a demonic plan!?

Japanese: その証人は、悪魔だったのです。
English: This woman IS a demon.

Japanese: そして‥‥その悪魔に魅入られた 協力者が、もうひとりいました。
English: And there was one more person who helped make a demon out of her.

Japanese: ‥‥美柳　勇希さん。 彼女の、お姉さんです。
English: Her sister... Valerie Hawthorne.

Japanese: ひ、被害者が‥‥ 誘拐事件の協力者ッ！
English: Y-You mean the victim was involved in the kidnapping plot as well!?

Japanese: 刑事である美柳　勇希が、 妹の犯罪に手を貸したというのか！
English: But she was a detective then! You're saying she participated in her sister's kidnapping!?

Japanese: そのとおりです！
English: Precisely!

Japanese: ‥‥きっと彼女は、この５年間、 苦しみつづけてきたのでしょう。
English: I'm sure that it weighed heavily on her conscience for the past 5 years.

Japanese: だからこそ‥‥ 今回の事件が起こった！
English: This is the sole reason behind the victim's murder.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: What do you mean by that?

Japanese: 事件当日‥‥
English: On the day of the murder...

Japanese: 尾並田に呼び出された被害者は、 美柳　ちなみに連絡した。
English: ...after receiving the phone call from Mr. Fawles, Valerie called her sister, Dahlia.

Japanese: そして、彼女に告げたのです。 ‥‥自らの《決意》を。
English: And then she told her what she was planning to do.

Japanese: ‥‥けつい‥‥とは‥‥？
English: "Planning to do"...?

Japanese: 《すべて、世間に公表する》‥‥ メモに書かれた、このコトバかッ！
English: She was going to tell the whole truth... As she wrote in her note!

Japanese: その行動が‥‥勇希さんの 運命を決定づけてしまった‥‥
English: THAT is what sealed Valerie Hawthorne's fate...

Japanese: あなたは一瞬にして、ふたたび 悪魔のような計画を練り上げた。
English: THAT is when you hatched your demonic plan to kill two birds with one stone.

Japanese: ５年前の狂言誘拐の協力者‥‥ ふたりの口を、同時にふさぐ！
English: A plan that would ensure neither of your accomplices to the kidnapping would talk.

Japanese: そのために、あなたは‥‥
English: And THAT is why...

Japanese: お姉さん‥‥美柳　勇希さんを 殺害したのです！
English: ...you killed your sister, Valerie Hawthorne!
Hee hee...

Japanese: だ、ダレですか！ こんなときに‥‥
English: Wh-Who is that!? Laughing at a time like this!

Japanese: ゴメンなさい。 なんか、おかしくて‥‥。
English: Forgive me. It's just hilarious...

Japanese: しょ‥‥証人‥‥？
English: W-Witness? Is that you?

Japanese: ‥‥おもしろいヒトね。 アヤサト　チヒロさん‥‥
English: You amuse me, woman! MS. MIA FEY...

Japanese: ずいぶんイセイのいい お話だったけど‥‥
English: You can certainly weave an exciting tale...

Japanese: トーゼン‥‥証拠は 見せてもらえるんでしょうねえ？
English: Naturally... you have the evidence to back it up, don't you?

Japanese: しょ、証拠‥‥？
English: Ev-Evidence...?

Japanese: わたしが、死刑囚や女刑事と 手を組んでいたこと‥‥
English: Evidence that I planned the kidnapping, of course...

Japanese: それを立証する証拠よ。 決まってるでしょ？
English: That at 14, I plotted it with Mr. Fawles and my sister.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥‥
English: W-Well, I...

Japanese: ああ。あと、もう１つ。
English: And one more thing.

Japanese: ‥‥２億円のダイヤが どうなったのか‥‥？
English: What happened to the 2 million dollar diamond?

Japanese: せめて、それぐらいは 立証してもらいませんとねえ‥‥
English: If you can't provide evidence to at least show that...

Japanese: ぬうううううううむ‥‥ 弁護人。いかがかな？
English: Hmmmmm... Well, Ms. Fey?

Japanese: わ‥‥‥わかりません‥‥ I...
English: I don't know...

Japanese: お話にならないわね。 アンタ‥‥バカじゃないの？
English: What a joke. YOU, MS. FEY... Are you stupid or something?
Nnrgh...!

Japanese: （５年前の狂言誘拐を、これ以上の カタチで立証するのは不可能！）
English: (How can I prove a fake kidnapping that happened 5 years ago!?)

Japanese: （美柳　勇希の殺害にも、 決定的な証拠は、何もない‥‥）
English: (I don't even have decisive proof of Valerie Hawthorne's murder...)

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ 結論が出たようですね。
English: Well it seems... that we've come to the end.

Japanese: 正直なところ、証人のタイドには ギモンが残りますが‥‥
English: To be honest, the witness's behavior does raise certain suspicions...

Japanese: 本法廷は、弁護側の主張を 却下せざるを得ません。
English: However, I am forced to reject the assertions made by the defense.

Japanese: トーゼン‥‥でしょ。
English: Of course you are.

Japanese: （これで‥‥ これで、おしまいなの‥‥？）
English: (Is this it...? Is it really over...?)

Japanese: （あんな子にバカにされて‥‥ 返すコトバがないなんて！）
English: (That girl has made a fool of me and there's nothing I can do about it!)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
証拠がなければ、審理はおしまい。 そんなコト、だれが決めた？
English: Without evidence the trial is over? Who decided that?

Japanese: か‥‥‥神乃木さん！
English: M-Mr. Armando!

Japanese: いいか、コネコちゃん。 ルールは、自分で決めるモンだぜ。
English: Come on now, Kitten. Haven't you figured out that you can make your own rules?

Japanese: ‥‥たとえ証拠がなくても‥‥ 証言という手がある！
English: For example, even if there's no evidence, there's still testimony!
(T-Testimony...?)

Japanese: 事件当日‥‥美柳　ちなみは 姉の美柳　勇希を殺害した。
English: On the day in question, Dahlia Hawthorne murdered her sister, Valerie Hawthorne.

Japanese: その死体をトランクにかくして、 おぼろ橋で尾並田に会った‥‥
English: She hid her body in the trunk of Mr. Fawles's stolen car and then went to meet with him...

Japanese: 姉・勇希になりすまして、な。 ‥‥そいつが、アンタの主張だ。
English: ...disguised as her sister, Valerie Hawthorne. That's what you think, right?

Japanese: そ、そうですけど‥‥
English: Y-Yes, that's right...

Japanese: それなら‥‥たったひとりだけ いるじゃねえか。
English: In that case, there's only one answer, right?

Japanese: その犯行を立証できる、 《証人》が‥‥！
English: There is only one person left who can testify about Valerie Hawthorne's murder!

Japanese: （証拠がない以上‥‥ 残された手段は、ただ１つ！）
English: (Since there's no proof, there's only one thing left to do!)

Japanese: （彼女の犯行を証言できる 《証人》とは‥‥？）
English: (Who is the one person who can testify to that demon woman's crimes...?)

Japanese: ‥‥いいか。こいつは 最後のチャンスなんだ。
English: Listen up. This is your last chance.

Japanese: もう一度‥‥ よく考えてみるんだな。
English: Think it over carefully one more time.

Japanese: （落ちつくの、千尋！ カンタンなコトでしょ‥‥！）
English: (Relax, Mia! This is easy...!)

Japanese: ‥‥裁判長！　弁護側は‥‥ 新たな証人を要請します！
English: Your Honor! The defense wishes to call a new witness!

Japanese: ‥‥証人‥‥ですか？
English: A new witness?

Japanese: 尾並田　美散‥‥彼の証言を 聞く必要があります！
English: Yes! We would like to hear the testimony of Terry Fawles!

Japanese: 被告人、だと‥‥！
English: The defendant...!?

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみが、この事件に 関わっているか、いないか‥‥？
English: There's only one person that can shed any further light on the situation...

Japanese: そのカギをにぎっているのは、 たった１つのポイントです。
English: Only one person that knows what Dahlia's role in the kidnapping was...

Japanese: この写真の人物は‥‥ 本当に《美柳　勇希》だったのか？
English: Only one person that can say whether the person in the photo is Valerie Hawthorne...

Japanese: それとも、姉になりすました 《美柳　ちなみ》だったのか‥‥？
English: ...or whether it was in fact her younger sister Dahlia, disguised as her.

Japanese: ‥‥それを証言できるのは、 たった１人しかいません。
English: There's only one person who can solve this riddle once and for all...

Japanese: ジッサイに彼女に会った人物‥‥ 尾並田　美散です！
English: And that person is... Terry Fawles!

Japanese: ‥‥いかがかな？ 御剣検事くん‥‥。
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth? What is your take on this?

Japanese: いいだろう。 ‥‥検察側に、異存はない。
English: Why not? The prosecution has no objection.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: Very well.

Japanese: 係官！　被告人を、証言台へ！
English: Bailiff! Bring the defendant to the witness stand!

Japanese: （これが‥‥最後の手段なの。 尾並田さん‥‥）
English: (This is my last chance, Mr. Fawles...)

Japanese: （美柳　ちなみの罪を 立証できるのは‥‥）
English: (My last chance to establish Dahlia's guilt...)

Japanese: （もう、あなたの証言しかない！）
English: (You're all I have left!)

Japanese: 今までのこと‥‥ 聞いていましたね、被告人。
English: Defendant, you've heard everything that's been said up to this point, yes?

Japanese: ‥‥うう‥‥オレ。 信じない。ゼッタイ。
English: Uhh... Umm... I don't believe it! No way!

Japanese: チナミ、死んだ！　５年前！ ウラ切ったの、ユウキ！
English: Dahlia died! Five years ago! Valerie betrayed me!

Japanese: 尾並田さん‥‥
English: Mr. Fawles...

Japanese: ５年前、彼女があなたに 何を言ったかは知りません。
English: I don't know what she said to you 5 years ago, but one thing is clear.

Japanese: でも‥‥彼女は生きていた。
English: Dahlia is very much alive.

Japanese: あなたは、利用されたの。 ２億円を手に入れるために‥‥
English: And you were used. For 2 million dollars.
That's... Not... True...

Japanese: ‥‥被告人。 問題は、たった１つです。
English: Mr. Fawles. There's only one question I want the answer to.

Japanese: あなたが２日前‥‥おぼろ橋の上で 会ったのは、だれだったか？
English: Two days ago on Dusky Bridge... who did you meet?

Japanese: 被害者の美柳　勇希だったのか？ 美柳　ちなみだったのか‥‥！
English: Was it Valerie Hawthorne? Or was it Dahlia Hawthorne?

Japanese: チナミ‥‥チナミ‥‥ オレを‥‥ウラ切った‥‥？
English: Dahlia... Dahlia... Did you... betray me?

Japanese: アイツ‥‥言ってた‥‥５年前。 オレたち、死ぬまで‥‥
English: Five years ago she promised... She promised... Never, ever betray each other...

Japanese: ‥‥ミチルさん‥‥
English: Terry...

Japanese: チナミ！
English: Dahlia!

Japanese: オマエ、やっぱり‥‥ 生きてたのか‥‥ッ！
English: It-It's true! You are alive...!

Japanese: わたしを‥‥お疑いなのね？ ‥‥しかたのないコトです。
English: You don't trust me anymore? That makes me sad.

Japanese: 教えてくれ！　ホントのコト！ オレ‥‥オマエ、信じてた‥‥
English: Tell the truth! The real truth! I... I believed in you!

Japanese: ‥‥‥コトバはいりませんわ。 あなたなら、わかるはず‥‥
English: I shouldn't NEED to say it. You should already know...

Japanese: ただ‥‥１つだけ、 ハッキリしています。
English: But... There is one thing that I WILL say.

Japanese: ‥‥わたしの運命は、 あなたがにぎっているの‥‥
English: My life is in your hands right now, Terry.
Dah...lia...

Japanese: 証言は、ただ一度。 ‥‥それ以上は認めません。
English: I will allow Mr. Fawles to testify once and once only.

Japanese: それでは、被告人。‥‥本法廷、 最後の証言をおねがいします！
English: Well then, Mr. Fawles. Yours will be the final testimony in this trial.

Japanese: 証人！
English: Witness!

Japanese: うがががああああああああああッ！
English: GAAAAAAAHHH!!!!

Japanese: ひゃああ！ ゴメンなさい！　ゴメンなさい！
English: Eeeeek! I'm sorry! I apologize!

Japanese: うぐぐぐぐ‥‥セ、センセイ！ ‥‥み、ミズ。ミズ、くれ‥‥
English: Uwah! Uwah! UWAAAAH!! W-Water! P-Please! Water!
Hmm?

Japanese: しゃべれねえ‥‥ ノド、カラカラ‥‥
English: Can't talk... Need water...

Japanese: クッ‥‥！　しかたねえ。 コーヒーをおごってやるぜ。
English: Ha...! Oh well, I guess it'll have to be my coffee instead.

Japanese: ‥‥これからアンタが吐く コトバにふさわしい‥‥
English: At least it'll match the way he's probably feeling right now...

Japanese: 地獄よりニガくて、 そして黒いコーヒーさ。
English: Darker and bitterer than Hell itself.

Japanese: うぐぐぐぐぐ‥‥
English: UWAAAAAH...

Japanese: ～尾並田が見た人物～
English: -- Who Terry Fawles Saw --

Japanese: あの日‥‥４時ごろ、おぼろ橋の たもとにクルマ、とめて‥‥
English: That day... 4 PM... I stopped the car. I was in front of bridge...

Japanese: まだ、アイツ、来てなかった。 だから‥‥橋の上で待った。
English: She wasn't there... So, I waited on bridge.

Japanese: そこからクルマ、見えてた。 死体なんか、だれも入れてない！
English: I watched my car from bridge. I never put no body in that car!

Japanese: やがて‥‥オンナがやってきた。 オレの目の前、立ち止まった。
English: Finally, one woman came. She stood front of me.

Japanese: オレたち。ハナシ、して‥‥ そのまま、別れた。
English: We talked... Then she left.

Japanese: あれは‥‥ゆ‥‥ゆ。ユウキ。 チナミじゃなかった！
English: That was... That was Valerie. Not my Dahlia!

Japanese: 尾並田さん！ ‥‥まだ、彼女をかばうの！
English: Mr. Fawles! You're covering for her!

Japanese: 彼女は、あなたのことを‥‥
English: Do you think she would do the same for you...!?

Japanese: ‥‥そこまでだ‥‥弁護士クン。
English: That's enough, Ms. Fey!

Japanese: 最後の証言らしく、 明快な内容だったようだ‥‥
English: His last statement was a fitting way to end the final testimony of the trial...

Japanese: それでは‥‥弁護人。 尋問をおねがいします。
English: Well then, Ms. Fey... Please proceed with your cross-examination.

Japanese: （尾並田さん‥‥それでいいの？）
English: (Is this how you want it to end, Mr. Fawles?)

Japanese: （死刑判決を受けて‥‥ 今、また有罪になるのよ！）
English: (Another guilty verdict to go along with your death sentence!?)

Japanese: それを止められるとしたら‥‥ アンタしかいねえ。‥‥たぶんな。
English: There's only one person who can stop it... You, Kitten. ...I think.

Japanese: ～尾並田が見た人物～
English: -- Who Terry Fawles Saw --

Japanese: あの日‥‥４時ごろ、おぼろ橋の たもとにクルマ、とめて‥‥
English: That day... 4 PM... I stopped the car. I was in front of bridge...

Japanese: まだ、アイツ、来てなかった。 だから‥‥橋の上で待った。
English: She wasn't there... So, I waited on bridge.

Japanese: そこからクルマ、見えてた。 死体なんか、だれも入れてない！
English: I watched my car from bridge. I never put no body in that car!

Japanese: やがて‥‥オンナがやってきた。 オレの目の前、立ち止まった。
English: Finally, one woman came. She stood front of me.

Japanese: オレたち。ハナシ、して‥‥ そのまま、別れた。
English: We talked... Then she left.

Japanese: あれは‥‥ゆ‥‥ゆ。ユウキ。 チナミじゃなかった！
English: That was... That was Valerie. Not my Dahlia!

Japanese: 言うまでもねえが‥‥ アイツはウソをついている。
English: I know it's obvious, but... he's clearly lying.

Japanese: 呪われてるのさ。 ‥‥美柳　ちなみに‥‥
English: He's been cursed... by Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: 死ぬまで、あの女を 信じようとするんだろうぜ。
English: He'll probably go to his grave still believing in her.

Japanese: 尾並田さん‥‥
English: Mr. Fawles...

Japanese: ヤツのヘタなウソを暴いても、 呪いは解けないだろうが‥‥
English: Even if you can show he's lying, the poor guy will still be cursed...

Japanese: それでも、ムジュンは 指摘しなけりゃならねえ。
English: You'll still have to point out the contradiction anyway.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、弁護士にかけられた 呪い、なんだからな‥‥
English: That's the curse of being a defense lawyer, I guess...

Japanese: メモによると、ヤクソクの 時間は、４時３０分。
English: According to the note, the meeting was supposed to take place at 4:30.

Japanese: ずいぶん早く 橋に行ったんですね？
English: You certainly arrived early, didn't you?

Japanese: パラパラ雨、降っていて‥‥ もう、暗かった。
English: It was raining... Already dark, too...

Japanese: ３０分間、橋の上で 待っていたわけですか？
English: You waited on the bridge for 30 minutes?

Japanese: 尾並田さん‥‥？
English: Mr. Fawles...?

Japanese: ‥‥吾童山‥‥思い出の場所。 オレ‥‥なつかしかった。
English: Eagle Mountain... That spot... Strong, strong memories...

Japanese: （なぜ、口ごもったのかしら‥‥）
English: (Why did he just clam up?)

Japanese: （もしかして‥‥ 何か、かくしているの‥‥？）
English: (Could it be... he's hiding something here?)

Japanese: あなたのほうが、先に 橋に行ったわけですね？
English: You were quite early, so you waited on the bridge, correct?

Japanese: オレ‥‥待つの、好き。 モンダイない。
English: Yeah... I like waiting. I'm used to it.

Japanese: ‥‥あのシマシマは、 ５年間も待っていたんだ。
English: I'm sure he is. Zebra Boy waited 5 years to ask a single question...

Japanese: 自分をウラ切ったオンナの コトバを、なァ。
English: To find out why a woman betrayed him.

Japanese: ‥‥３０分ぐらい、まばたきする ヒマもなかっただろうぜ。
English: To him, 30 minutes must've been like a blink of the eye.

Japanese: ‥‥クルマのほうを 見ていたんですか？
English: You were watching the car?

Japanese: あの橋。反対側、コワれてた。 そっちからヒト、来られない。
English: That bridge. Other side is broken. Nobody can come from there.

Japanese: だから‥‥クルマ、見ていた。
English: So... I was watching car.

Japanese: フッ‥‥。 トーゼンのコト、だな‥‥
English: *sigh*... What else were you expecting him to do...?

Japanese: （たしかに、トーゼンだけど‥‥）
English: (I suppose that's the obvious thing to do, but...)

Japanese: （‥‥ちょっと引っかかる。 この感じ‥‥ムジュン？）
English: (...something's bothering me. I'm getting that feeling... A contradiction?)

Japanese: こわれた橋の向こう側には、 何があるのですかな？
English: I wonder what's on the other side of the broken bridge anyway?

Japanese: ヒトは住んでいない。
English: No one lives there.

Japanese: 山の中に、小さな お堂があるだけ、だ‥‥
English: There's a small shrine up on the mountain, but that's it...

Japanese: とにかく‥‥！　クルマ、 ダレも近寄らなかった！
English: Anyway...! Nobody came... No car, nothing!

Japanese: ‥‥尾並田さん。 よく考えてください。
English: Mr. Fawles. Think carefully now.

Japanese: 本当に‥‥美柳　勇希さん だったんですか？
English: Are you certain that it was Valerie Hawthorne?

Japanese: う。う。う‥‥
English: Uh. Uh. Uh...

Japanese: ウソ、つかない！ オレ、ホントのコト言う！
English: I never lie! It-It's the truth!

Japanese: あれは‥‥ユウキ！ オレ‥‥カオ、おぼえてた！
English: It was... Valerie! I remembered her face!

Japanese: “カオをおぼえていた”‥‥？
English: Wait a minute. If you had remembered her face...

Japanese: でも‥‥目印にマフラーを 用意させたじゃないですか。
English: ...then why did you make her wear a scarf as identification?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥う。 ゴメン。ちょっと、ウソついた。
English: Uh. Sorry. I told a little lie.

Japanese: でも！　あのとき会ったの‥‥ さっきのオンナじゃなかった！
English: But! The woman I met... She was different from woman standing here now!

Japanese: ‥‥ベツのオンナ！ ユウキだった！
English: She was different! It was Valerie!

Japanese: 彼女と、 なんの話をしたのですか？
English: What did you talk to her about anyway?

Japanese: 尾並田さん！
English: Mr. Fawles!

Japanese: ユウキ、オレに言った！
English: Valerie told the truth. About the kidnapping 5 years ago.

Japanese: ５年前の事件‥‥『ダレかに セキニンをとらせたかった』‥‥
English: She said, "Someone needed to take the blame for it"...

Japanese: 『それしか考えられなかった』 ‥‥彼女、そう言ってた。
English: "That was all I could think to do." She said that.

Japanese: だから、ウソの証言で オレを死刑に‥‥。
English: That's why she lied... Got me the death penalty...

Japanese: ‥‥証人は、そのコタエで マンゾクしたと言うのかな？
English: And were you satisfied with that answer, witness?

Japanese: チナミが死んだの‥‥ オレのせい。
English: Dahlia died... It was my fault.

Japanese: よくおぼえてないけど‥‥オレ。
English: But I don't really remember...

Japanese: あのとき、チナミを‥‥ 川につき落としたのかもしれない。
English: Maybe I did... Maybe I did push her in...

Japanese: 今となっては‥‥もう、 どうでもよかった。
English: It don't matter no more...

Japanese: ダイジなのは‥‥ チナミ、もういない。それだけ。
English: Either way... My Dahlia... My sweet Teen Angel... Dead.

Japanese: （その美柳　ちなみは‥‥ 生きていたというのに！）
English: (But you just saw that she isn't dead!)

Japanese: 橋の上で、ユウキのハナシ聞いて ‥‥最後のココロ残りも消えた。
English: After Valerie talk to me on bridge... Nothing left to live for...

Japanese: どうして断言できるんですか！
English: How can you be so sure!?

Japanese: 事件当日は、雨がふっていた。 それに、当日の日没は、午後５時。
English: It was raining at the time, and sunset that day was at 5 o'clock.

Japanese: ４時３０分なら、現場はすでに うす暗かったはずです！ at 4:30!
English: It would have already been pretty dark on that mountain

Japanese: 尾並田さん、おねがい！ 最後のチャンスなんですよ！
English: Please Mr. Fawles! This is your last chance!

Japanese: これ以上、無実の罪を 背負いこまないで！
English: You've already taken the fall once for something you didn't do!

Japanese: あのオンナは‥‥ チナミじゃなかった。
English: That woman... It wasn't Dahlia.

Japanese: そこまでだ！ ‥‥カンペキな回答だな。
English: Stop right there! What more needs to be said!?

Japanese: ぬううううううううぐ。
English: Hmmmmm.

Japanese: （死刑になっても、殺人の罪を 着せられても‥‥）
English: (Even if it means the death penalty. Even if it means taking the blame for murder...)

Japanese: （まだ、彼女を守ろうと言うの？ ‥‥尾並田さん）
English: (You'll still do whatever is necessary to protect her... Won't you, Mr. Fawles?)

Japanese: おぼろ橋に行ったとき‥‥ 《彼女》は、まだ来ていなかった。
English: So when you got to the bridge, no one had arrived, huh?

Japanese: あなたは、橋の上で待っていた‥‥ まちがいありませんか？
English: So you waited on the bridge... You're sure of that?

Japanese: マチガイない。
English: Yeah. I'm sure.

Japanese: そう‥‥。まちがい ありませんね、尾並田さん。
English: You're sure, huh...? Well then I'm sure too, Mr. Fawles...

Japanese: あなたは、ウソをついている。
English: I'm sure that you're lying.
Huh! Uhh... Wah...

Japanese: えへん！　‥‥えへん、えへん。
English: Wah! Wah! WAAAH!!

Japanese: ‥‥聞かせてもらおうか。 弁護人‥‥
English: Oh? I would love to hear your rationale on this, Ms. Fey...

Japanese: “どちらが先に、 おぼろ橋に来たのか‥‥”？
English: You want to know who arrived at the bridge first?

Japanese: ‥‥この写真を見れば ハッキリわかります。
English: Just look at this photo. It's perfectly clear.

Japanese: どう考えても‥‥、先に来たほうが 橋の奥にいるはずですよね‥‥？
English: Obviously the person that came first would be the one at the end of the bridge, right?

Japanese: し、しかし‥‥奥にいるのは、 《被害者》の方ではないかッ！
English: B-But that's the victim at the end of the bridge!

Japanese: そのとおりです。 つまり、尾並田さん‥‥
English: Precisely my point. In other words, Mr. Fawles...

Japanese: あなたは、《彼女》よりもあとに、 橋についたことになるの。
English: You must have arrived at the bridge after she did.
Nng... Urkkk... Ahhh... Ggggaah... Blah...

Japanese: ねえ、尾並田さん。
English: Umm, Mr. Fawles.

Japanese: もう、ムリをしないで‥‥ 本当のことを聞かせてほしいの。
English: Please don't get so worked up. We just want the truth.

Japanese: 橋のたもとについたの、４時。 まちがいない。
English: I got there around 4 o'clock. It's true.

Japanese: オレ‥‥行くところがあった。 ヤクソクの場所‥‥
English: I... I had somewhere to go. A special place...

Japanese: おぼろ橋に行く前に、 そちらへ向かったわけですか‥‥
English: Did you go to this special place before you went to the bridge?

Japanese: 橋から１５分ぐらい歩いたところ、 古い山寺がある。
English: Yeah... It's an old temple about 15 minutes from the bridge.

Japanese: そこで‥‥５年前。 チナミと、ヤクソクした。
English: Five years ago, me and Dahlia... We promised each other...

Japanese: 死ぬまで、おたがいを ウラ切らない、‥‥と。
English: We swore we wouldn't betray each other...

Japanese: おたがいを信じ抜くために‥‥ 《アカシ》‥‥用意した。
English: She brought a memento... To represent... our love.

Japanese: 《アカシ》‥‥というのは？
English: A memento...?

Japanese: ５年前。境内の木のたもとに、 ソイツ、かくしておいた。
English: Five years ago, I hid it under base of tree there.

Japanese: なつかしい、タイセツな思い出。 これを‥‥オレ。取りに行った。
English: It's a special memory for me. This is it... This is what I went to get.

Japanese: 《アカシ》というのは‥‥ そのペンダントですか。
English: This little bottle on a necklace is your memento?

Japanese: キレイな小ビンですな。 ‥‥カラッポのようですが。
English: It's quite charming... but it looks empty.

Japanese: 裁判長！ ‥‥お聞きのとおりです。
English: Your Honor! You heard what my client said.

Japanese: 被告人は、午後４時に現場に ついてから‥‥
English: He arrived at the scene at 4 o'clock.

Japanese: クルマから一度、 はなれているのです！
English: But he then left his car unattended and walked away!

Japanese: そして‥‥往復３０分ちかくの キョリを歩いた！
English: He was gone for approximately 30 minutes!

Japanese: ぐ‥‥ッ！
English: Urk...!

Japanese: これだけの時間があれば‥‥
English: With that much time...

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみは、死体をトランクに かくすことができた！
English: ...Dahlia Hawthorne could have easily hidden the body in the trunk of his car!

Japanese: ぐおおおおおおおおおおおおおッ！
English: N-NOOOOOOO!

Japanese: たしかに‥‥その時間は じゅうぶん、あったでしょう！
English: Indeed... There certainly was enough time for it!

Japanese: （ついに‥‥ チャンスをつかんだ！）
English: (I've still got a chance!)

Japanese: 尾並田さん！ まちがいありませんよね！
English: Mr. Fawles! There's no mistaking it!

Japanese: ‥‥げふっ！
English: Urp!

Japanese: お、尾並田さん‥‥？
English: Huh? Mr. Fawles...!?

Japanese: もう‥‥じゅうぶん‥‥ センセイ。
English: Th-That's enough... Please...

Japanese: しょ、証人‥‥？
English: W-Witness?

Japanese: オレたち、ヤクソクした。 ‥‥５年前‥‥。
English: I-I promised her... 5 years ago...

Japanese: もし、これから先‥‥
English: If it ever happens...

Japanese: おたがいが信じられなくなったら、 これ‥‥
English: ...that we can't trust each other no more... Then...

Japanese: この小ビンの中身‥‥ 飲みほす‥‥って‥‥
English: ...we're supposed to... drink... bottle... Ugh...

Japanese: ば‥‥バカな！ 裁判長！　ただちに、休廷を！
English: N-No! Stop the trial! Your Honor! We need a recess!

Japanese: ‥‥オレ‥‥バカだから。 ヤクソク、守れそうもない‥‥
English: I... I was stupid... Couldn't... keep... promise...

Japanese: だから‥‥これ、使った。
English: So I did it... I... drank... this...

Japanese: ダメ！　もう少しなのよ！ もう少しで‥‥
English: No! We are so close! Just a little more...

Japanese: あなたの無実が立証できるの！ だから‥‥
English: I was going to prove your innocence!

Japanese: オレ‥‥無罪になったら‥‥ 自信がない‥‥
English: No... Don't want that... Don't... trust... self...

Japanese: チナミを‥‥もう、一度‥‥ 殺してしまう‥‥かも‥‥
English: Maybe kill again... Kill sweet Dahlia... again...

Japanese: ‥‥尾並田さん！
English: Mr. Fawles!

Japanese: センセイ‥‥
English: Mr. Ar... Armando...

Japanese: コーヒー‥‥ ど‥‥も‥‥ありがと‥‥
English: Th-Thanks... For the... coffee...

Japanese: 尾並田さああああああああああん！
English: MR. FAAAAAAAAWLES!!!!

Japanese: こうして‥‥初めての裁判は 突然、終わった。
English: And so my first trial ended... Suddenly, and tragically.

Japanese: 判決が言いわたされることも、 勝利も敗北もないまま‥‥
English: It ended with no winners... Only losers.

Japanese: 私のココロには、いやしがたい 大きなキズだけが残った。
English: I ended up with a wound that cut so deep into my soul, I thought it'd never heal.

Japanese: それはきっと‥‥ この若い検事さんも同じだろう。
English: I'm sure it was the same for the young prosecutor as well.

Japanese: ‥‥そして‥‥
English: But one person...

Japanese: 真犯人・美柳　ちなみは‥‥
English: The true criminal, Dahlia Hawthorne...

Japanese: 美しくほほえむと、静かに 法廷をあとにしたのだった。
English: She left the courtroom with a secret smile on her demonically sweet face.

Japanese: ‥‥ユルせねえ。
English: Unforgivable... That witch...

Japanese: か、神乃木さん‥‥
English: M-Mr. Armando...

Japanese: 真実は、もうそこに見えていた。
English: We were so close to the truth. It was right there in front of us.

Japanese: つかみそこねたのは‥‥ ただ、アマかったからだ。
English: You were just a little too soft, Kitten...

Japanese: 私のせいです！　‥‥私のせいで、 尾並田さんは‥‥うううう‥‥
English: It's my fault! It's all my fault that Mr. Fawles killed himself!

Japanese: 泣くな。‥‥コーヒーが しょっぱくなっちまうぜ‥‥。
English: Don't cry, Kitten. You're going to make my coffee all salty.

Japanese: ううう‥‥やっぱり‥‥ 私なんかには、ムリだったんです！
English: I-I knew it...! I kn-knew I wasn't cut out for this...!

Japanese: チヒロ‥‥
English: Mia...

Japanese: ‥‥わからねえのか？ 今はまだ、泣くときじゃねえ。
English: Don't you get it? You can't cry yet.

Japanese: オトコが泣いていいのは‥‥ すべてを終えたときだけ、だぜ。
English: The only time a lawyer can cry is when it's all over.

Japanese: か、神乃木さん‥‥
English: M-Mr. Armando...

Japanese: ‥‥それが、どんな事件であれ‥‥
English: No matter how tough the case... No matter how bitter the memories...

Japanese: やがては色あせて、ファイルされて ‥‥いつしか、忘れられる。
English: ...they always fade over time. Then you file them away and eventually forget them...

Japanese: この法廷から、１年後‥‥
English: One year later, in this very same courthouse...

Japanese: ぼくは、“あの事件”に まきこまれたんだ‥‥。
English: I, myself, got wrapped up in "that case".

Japanese: ‥‥あのあと、美柳　ちなみは 正式な審理を受けた。
English: Only after that, did Dahlia Hawthorne get put on trial for her crimes.

Japanese: ‥‥彼女に対する 最終的な判決は‥‥
English: The verdict that was ultimately handed down to her was...

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、《有罪》だった。
English: "Guilty", of course.

Japanese: その瞬間、彼女はやはり、 美しくほほえんだという。
English: Naturally, when the verdict was read, she had a perfect, angelic smile on her face.

Japanese: ‥‥それで、すべては終わった。 少なくとも、ぼくはそう思った。
English: It was finally all over. At least, that's what I thought at the time.

Japanese: しかし‥‥
English: Unfortunately...

Japanese: そうではなかったことを、 ぼくは、今‥‥
English: I couldn't have been more wrong...

Japanese: あれから５年もたった、今‥‥ 思い知らされることになる。
English: It's been 5 years, but now something has happened that's made me remember all this.

Japanese: ■第４話　始まりの逆転 おわり
English: Episode 4: Turnabout Beginnings THE END
